SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.messagebus

Class MessageDispatcherRunner

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.messagebus.MessageDispatcherRunner
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class MessageDispatcherRunner extends Object implements Runnable
The dispatcher thread for the Message Bus process.

This is the second of the two threads that make up the entire Message Bus process (the first being the Netty acceptor managed by MessageBusServerRunner):

  Netty worker thread(s)           MessageDispatcherRunner
  ───────────────────────          ───────────────────────
  channelRead0() called
  → deserialise JSON frame
  → MessageBus.enqueueInbound()   ← blocks on queue.take()
  → return immediately             → MessageBus.publish()
                                   → channel.writeAndFlush() per subscriber

The Netty I/O threads never do fan-out. They deserialise the frame, place it on the BlockingQueue inside MessageBus, and return. This thread is the only one that calls MessageBus.publish(Message, String), so there is no contention on the fan-out path.

Serialisation of the outbound JSON string (needed for the MessageBus.publish(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.Message<?>, java.lang.String) call) is done here on this thread, not on the Netty I/O thread, keeping I/O threads as lightweight as possible.

This thread blocks on MessageBus.takeInbound() when the queue is empty, consuming no CPU until a message arrives. It exits cleanly when interrupted or when stop() is called.

Usage: 

   MessageBus messageBus = new MessageBus();

   // Thread 1: Netty acceptor
   MessageBusServerRunner server = new MessageBusServerRunner(messageBus, 9090);
   new Thread(server, "msgbus-acceptor").start();

   // Thread 2: dispatcher
   MessageDispatcherRunner dispatcher = new MessageDispatcherRunner(messageBus);
   new Thread(dispatcher, "msgbus-dispatcher").start();

  • Constructor Details

    • MessageDispatcherRunner

      public MessageDispatcherRunner(MessageBus messageBus)
      Constructs the dispatcher.
      Parameters:
      messageBus - the message bus to drain and dispatch from

  • Method Details

    • run

      public void run()
      Continuously takes messages from the inbound queue and fans them out to all subscribers of the message's topic.

      For each message:

      1. Blocks on MessageBus.takeInbound() until a message is available.
      2. Serialises the message to a JSON string on this thread (not on the Netty I/O thread that enqueued it).
      3. Calls MessageBus.publish(Message, String) which writes the serialised string to each active subscriber channel via channel.writeAndFlush() — a non-blocking Netty operation.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the dispatch loop. The thread will exit after its current MessageBus.takeInbound() unblocks (either from a queued message or from interruption).
